commands.author: Jhacarreiro
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=cb46de5e317baa59eb0c022211da3868be3ad747)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Discord `probeDiscord()` `/users/@me` success parsing to use `readProviderJsonResponse`, adds an oversized-stream regression test, and adds a loopback proof script.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +37, Other +144. Total +185 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection rather than local execution: current main and v2026.6.10 still call native `res.json()` in the Discord probe success path. The PR also supplies loopback `node:http` after-fix proof through exported `probeDiscord()`.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the remaining action is ordinary maintainer review plus optional proof-transcript cleanup.

target.title: fix(cron): add opt-in billing guard probes
target.body:
## Summary

This PR changes the cron billing safety behavior from an unconditional stop into an opt-in billing guard for recurring `agentTurn` cron jobs.

Default behavior is unchanged. Unless `cron.billingGuard.enabled` is explicitly set, recurring jobs continue to use the existing retry/backoff behavior.

When the guard is enabled and a recurring `agentTurn` run records `lastErrorReason === "billing"`, the scheduler now:

- keeps the job enabled;
- schedules the next run using the billing guard probe backoff;
- logs a warning that a billing guard probe was scheduled;
- returns to the normal recurring schedule automatically after a successful run.

## What Problem This Solves

Recurring scheduled agent jobs can repeatedly retry after provider-side billing failures. Billing failures are usually not fixed by immediately retrying the same request, but permanently disabling the job after a single error is too aggressive for existing users.

This PR avoids both failure modes:

- no default compatibility break for existing recurring cron jobs;
- opt-in deployments can slow billing-classified retries into probe cadence and recover automatically when billing/provider access is fixed.

## Behavior

Without opt-in:

```text
recurring agentTurn cron + billing-classified error
=> existing recurring retry/backoff behavior is preserved
```

With opt-in:

```json5
{
  cron: {
    billingGuard: {
      enabled: true,
      probeBackoffMs: [1800000, 3600000, 7200000]
    }
  }
}
```

```text
recurring agentTurn cron + billing-classified error
=> enabled remains true
=> nextRunAtMs is delayed by billingGuard.probeBackoffMs
=> a later successful run clears lastErrorReason and consecutiveErrors
=> normal recurring schedule resumes
```

The guard only applies to recurring `agentTurn` jobs and only when the error is classified as `billing`. Rate-limit-only failures keep the existing retry/backoff path.

## Evidence

Validation performed on this branch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.billing-safety.test.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       68 passed (68)
```

Additional validation:

```bash
git diff --check
```

Result: no whitespace/check errors.

Focused coverage now verifies:

- default billing behavior is preserved when the guard is not enabled;
- opt-in billing guard schedules a probe without disabling the recurring job;
- rate-limit-only failures keep the existing retry/backoff behavior;
- successful billing guard probes recover automatically and return the job to the normal recurring schedule.

## Why This Replaces The Previous Approach

The earlier version disabled recurring `agentTurn` jobs after one billing-classified failure. That avoided repeated requests, but it changed the default recurring cron contract and required manual operator recovery after billing was fixed.

This version keeps the default contract compatible and makes the safer behavior explicit opt-in with automatic recovery.

## Scope

Changed files:

- `src/config/types.cron.ts`
- `src/cron/service/timer.ts`
- `src/cron/service/timer.billing-safety.test.ts`
- `docs/automation/cron-jobs.md`
- `docs/cli/cron.md`

## Runtime proof: `cron.billingGuard` accepts config, probes, and recovers

Redacted proof was run against PR head `6488fee769e28a4c561ea2b5fa7ad1f33662c340` using a temporary runtime harness and temporary cron state. No production `/data/.openclaw/openclaw.json`, credentials, IPs, phone numbers, or non-public endpoints were used or modified.

```console
COMMAND: run redacted runtime proof for OpenClaw PR95787
{
  "proof": "openclaw-95787-runtime-billing-guard-redacted",
  "repo": "openclaw/openclaw",
  "head": "6488fee769e28a4c561ea2b5fa7ad1f33662c340",
  "productionConfigTouched": false,
  "configSchemaAccepted": true,
  "configuredBillingGuard": {
    "enabled": true,
    "probeBackoffMs": [300000]
  },
  "billingErrorProbe": {
    "jobEnabledAfterBillingError": true,
    "lastErrorReasonAfterBillingError": "billing",
    "consecutiveErrorsAfterBillingError": 1,
    "expectedProbeAt": "2026-01-01T00:05:01.000Z",
    "actualProbeAt": "2026-01-01T00:05:01.000Z"
  },
  "maintenancePreservation": {
    "maintenanceChanged": false,
    "probeBeforeMaintenance": "2026-01-01T00:05:01.000Z",
    "probeAfterMaintenance": "2026-01-01T00:05:01.000Z"
  },
  "recoveryAfterSuccessfulProbe": {
    "jobEnabledAfterSuccess": true,
    "lastErrorReasonAfterSuccess": "undefined",
    "consecutiveErrorsAfterSuccess": 0,
    "nextRunAfterSuccess": "2026-01-01T00:06:00.000Z"
  }
}
```

Validation still passing locally after the latest code revision:

```console
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.jobs.test.ts src/cron/service/timer.billing-safety.test.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts
# 228/228 passed

node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
# passed

git diff --check
# passed
```
